SEARCH
Package com.mps.deepviolet.api.tls

Class ClientHello

java.lang.Object
com.mps.deepviolet.api.tls.ClientHello
public class ClientHello extends Object
Builder for TLS ClientHello messages. Generates ClientHello bytes based on ClientHelloConfig. The ClientHello structure (RFC 5246 / RFC 8446): - handshake type: 1 byte (0x01 = ClientHello) - length: 3 bytes - version: 2 bytes (legacy, always 0x0303 for TLS 1.3) - random: 32 bytes - session_id: 1 byte length + data - cipher_suites: 2 byte length + list - compression_methods: 1 byte length + list - extensions: 2 byte length + list

    Get the client random bytes (32 bytes).

    Get the EC key pair used for key_share extension (TLS 1.3, secp256r1).
    int
    getRecordVersion()
    Get the record layer version to use when sending this ClientHello.

    Get the session ID.
    KeyPair

    Get the X25519 key pair used for key_share extension (TLS 1.3).

    Set custom client random bytes.
    void

    Set session ID (for session resumption testing).

  • Method Details

    • getClientRandom

      public byte[] getClientRandom()
      Get the client random bytes (32 bytes).

    • setClientRandom

      public void setClientRandom(byte[] random)
      Set custom client random bytes.

    • getSessionId

      public byte[] getSessionId()
      Get the session ID.

    • setSessionId

      public void setSessionId(byte[] id)
      Set session ID (for session resumption testing).

    • getKeyShareKeyPair

      public KeyPair getKeyShareKeyPair()
      Get the EC key pair used for key_share extension (TLS 1.3, secp256r1). Returns null if key_share was not included.

    • getX25519KeyPair

      public KeyPair getX25519KeyPair()
      Get the X25519 key pair used for key_share extension (TLS 1.3). Returns null if X25519 key share was not included.

    • build

      public byte[] build()
      Build the ClientHello message bytes.
      Returns:
      Complete ClientHello handshake message

    • getRecordVersion

      public int getRecordVersion()
      Get the record layer version to use when sending this ClientHello. For TLS 1.3 compatibility, use TLS 1.0 or 1.2 as the record version.
